title: lenticula minima
intro: Lenticula minima, also known as the lesser wood sorrel, is a small, perennial plant native to Europe, which belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Lenticula minima include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a diuretic. Lenticula minima can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
